items.title: What is Chisto.mk?
items.content: Chisto.mk is a civic environmental platform for Macedonia. With the mobile app on iOS and Android you photograph pollution sites, pin them on a public map, follow updates, and join cleanup events when organisers publish them.

items.title: Is the app free?
items.content: Yes, and it will stay free. Creating an account, submitting reports, using the map, and joining cleanups cost nothing. There are no subscriptions, in-app purchases, or hidden fees. Chisto.mk is built as a public civic tool, not a paid product.

items.title: Who sees my reports?
items.content: After moderation, approved reports appear on the public map with the photo and location you submitted. Communities, journalists, and partners use the map to verify sites and track progress. We explain what personal data is shown in our privacy policy.

items.title: Can I report anonymously?
items.content: You need a verified account to submit reports, so we can reduce spam and follow up when needed. Your full name is not shown on every screen, but reports are linked to your account and may be visible to moderators. Read the privacy policy for details.

items.title: How do cleanup events work?
items.content: Organisers publish cleanup events in the app, usually linked to a pollution site on the map. Browse nearby events, register when signup is open, check in on the day, and see outcomes after the activity finishes.

items.title: How can organisations partner with you?
items.content: Municipalities, NGOs, schools, and companies can contact us to explore map data, joint cleanups, or awareness campaigns. Use the contact form and we will reply with next steps.

cta.subtitle: Document a pollution site once from your phone. Neighbours and organisers may already be tracking the same place.

creators.name: Ivan Mitkovski
creators.title: CTO
creators.imageAlt: Portrait of Ivan Mitkovski, CTO of Chisto.mk
bioParagraphs: Born in Skopje in 2004, Ivan is a classically trained guitarist from the State Music School "Ilija Nikolovski - Luj."

He plays guitar in a band with Vasil Angelov, and has always loved both music and technology. He once hoped to play violin, but with so many violinists in his generation, destiny chose the guitar for him.
He has worked on several significant programming projects, and leads engineering at Chisto.mk as CTO with the same care he brings to music.

creators.name: Vasil Angelov
creators.title: CMO
creators.affiliation: Member of the Executive Board of EKOHAB Skopje
creators.imageAlt: Portrait of Vasil Angelov, CMO of Chisto.mk
bioParagraphs: Born in Skopje in 2003, Vasil grew up in a family of musicians and chose music with intention.

He trained at the State Music School "Ilija Nikolovski - Luj," starting with percussion before classical solo singing. That foundation shaped his discipline, while his path moved toward contemporary pop.
He plays guitar, piano, drums, and bass, and works in audio and video production. He liked the idea behind Chisto.mk and joined as CMO, ready to help as a Principal Marketer.

creators.name: Leon Mitev
creators.title: Senior Marketer
creators.affiliation: President of the Supervisory Board of EKOHAB Skopje
creators.imageAlt: Portrait of Leon Mitev, Senior Marketer at Chisto.mk
bioParagraphs: Leon Mitev is a Senior Marketer at Chisto.mk, working one tier below Principal Marketer on the marketing team.
He is President of the Supervisory Board of EKOHAB Skopje, and helps carry the platform's voice with care so more people can find Chisto.mk.
Alongside Vasil Angelov, he treats marketing as craft: clear, human, and built to grow a civic product with trust.

creators.name: Antonio Hristovski
creators.title: CDO
creators.affiliation: Member of the Executive Board of EKOHAB Skopje
creators.imageAlt: Portrait of Antonio Hristovski, CDO of Chisto.mk
bioParagraphs: Antonio Hristovski is Chief Design Officer at Chisto.mk, the highest design seat on the team.
He designed the full product experience: the landing site, mobile app, admin tools, branding, and the design system that holds them together.
As a member of the Executive Board of EKOHAB Skopje, he gives Chisto.mk a calm, coherent face across every surface.

sections.title: 3. Personal data we collect
sections.body:
We collect personal data to run the civic map, authenticate users, moderate content, and improve reliability. Categories include:

3.1 Data you provide directly

3.1.1 Account data (apps)

First and last name
Email address
Mobile phone number (used for sign-in and verification with one-time codes)
Password (we store a secure hash, not the plain password)
Optional profile photo and in-app preferences
Optional multi-factor authentication data if you enable it
Gamification fields tied to your account (for example points), where the product includes them

3.1.2 Reports and sites

When you submit a pollution report we collect the report text, category and similar fields you enter, photos you attach, coordinates and address or place text for the location, timestamps, and moderation outcomes. Reports are linked to a "site" record on the map. Other users may see public report and site content according to product rules.

3.1.3 Community and events

Comments and likes on sites, saves, and share actions you trigger
Cleanup events shown in the product (scheduled time, linked site, organiser fields the UI collects, participant counts)

3.1.4 Support and contact

What you send us by email, contact forms, or comparable channels

3.1.5 Payments

We do not operate in-app purchases or paid subscriptions in the current product. If we introduce paid features later, we will update this Policy and name the payment processor.

3.2 Data collected automatically

3.2.1 Technical and security data

IP address, device type, operating system and app or browser version where available
Session or device records we need for security (for example refresh token identifiers, optional device description)
Server and application logs for reliability and abuse detection

3.2.2 Usage data

Interactions needed to operate features you use (for example opening a report, voting, commenting)
Optional product analytics if implemented in the apps (we will describe the vendor here when enabled)

3.2.3 Location

Precise location when you choose to attach a location to a report or use map features that require it. We do not intend to track your location continuously in the background; if that ever changes, we will ask for a separate permission and update this Policy.

3.2.4 Push notifications

If you opt in, we store a push device token (for example via Firebase Cloud Messaging) and related preference flags so we can deliver notifications you request.

3.2.5 Website cookies and storage

The public website uses strictly necessary storage plus, if you agree, Vercel Web Analytics. Details are in the Cookie Policy.

3.3 Third-party sources

We may use mapping or geocoding services (for example Carto basemaps or Esri imagery in the apps, and geocoding helpers) to render maps or resolve addresses; those providers may receive technical requests needed to load tiles or geocode coordinates. We do not operate social login in the current apps; if we add it, we will update this section.

sections.title: 4. Legal basis for processing
sections.body:
Under GDPR Article 6, we rely on:

4.1 Contract (Art. 6(1)(b))

Operating accounts, publishing reports you submit, showing map content, comments, events, and notifications you ask for.

4.2 Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f))

Securing the service, preventing abuse, debugging and reliability, internal statistics that do not require marketing profiles, and limited product improvement, balanced against your rights.

4.3 Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c))

Accounting, tax, or regulatory duties where they apply, and lawful requests from authorities.

4.4 Consent (Art. 6(1)(a))

Where required, for example optional marketing communications, optional website analytics cookies, or any future feature we offer only on an opt-in basis. You may withdraw consent without affecting earlier processing that was lawful.

4.5 Vital interests (Art. 6(1)(d))

Rare processing needed to protect life or serious safety.

sections.title: 5. How we use your personal data
sections.body:
5.1 Running the Platform

Authenticate you, maintain your profile, and operate map, report, comment, event, and notification features you use.

5.2 Safety, trust, and moderation

Review reports of abuse, enforce our Terms, protect users, and keep infrastructure stable.

5.3 Communications

Send service messages (for example security notices or report status) using contact details you provide. Marketing or newsletter messages only where we have a lawful basis (usually consent).

5.4 Aggregates for partners

Produce statistics that do not identify individuals where we describe in agreements or product copy.

5.5 Legal and compliance

Meet retention duties, respond to lawful requests, and defend legal claims.

5.6 Improvement

Understand errors and performance in aggregate; we avoid unnecessary profiling beyond what the product needs.

sections.title: 6. Data sharing and disclosure
sections.body:
6.1 Consent or instruction

We share data with third parties when you ask us to or when you deliberately make content public in the product.

6.2 Infrastructure and operations

We use vetted processors, for example:

Hosting and API infrastructure: [HOSTING_PROVIDER] in [SERVER_LOCATION] (or as updated in our records)
Email delivery for website contact forms and update notifications: [EMAIL_PROVIDER]
SMS verification: Twilio where SMS one-time codes are active
Push notifications: Google Firebase Cloud Messaging for device delivery
Mobile diagnostics and analytics: Firebase Crashlytics and Firebase Analytics when included in the production app
Website analytics (if you opt in on the site): Vercel Web Analytics
Support channel: [SUPPORT_PROVIDER]

Contracts require processors to use data only on our instructions and to apply appropriate security.

6.3 Maps and geolocation helpers

Map tiles may be loaded from Carto (OpenStreetMap-based raster tiles) and, where the UI offers it, Esri World Imagery or similar imagery providers. Geocoding may use platform APIs you already bundle. Those providers receive technical requests (tile coordinates, IP in server logs) as normal for map delivery.

6.4 Authorities and aggregates

We may share anonymised or aggregated environmental statistics with municipalities or partners. We may disclose identifiers or content if the law compels us, to protect rights and safety, or to enforce our Terms.

6.5 Reorganisation or succession

If we merge, restructure, or another organisation continues the service, personal data may transfer to the successor. We will require them to respect this Policy or notify you.

6.6 Public visibility

Reports, comments, usernames, and event listings you publish may be visible to other users and, depending on configuration, on the open web.

sections.title: 7. International data transfers
sections.body:
7.1 Data storage locations

Your personal data is primarily stored on servers located in [SERVER_COUNTRY/REGION].

7.2 Transfers outside EU/EEA

If we transfer data to countries outside the European Economic Area that do not have an adequacy decision from the European Commission, we ensure appropriate safeguards:

EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
Binding Corporate Rules (where applicable)
Recipient's certification under approved frameworks
Your explicit consent for specific transfers

7.3 Macedonian context

As a candidate country for EU membership, the Republic of Macedonia aligns its data protection framework with EU standards. We treat data protection with the same rigor as EU member states.

sections.title: 8. Data retention
sections.body:
We keep personal data only as long as needed for the purposes above or as the law requires. Exact schedules evolve with the product; the following reflects our current intent.

8.1 Accounts and security

Profile and credentials: while your account is active, then typically up to 90 days after closure unless law or dispute resolution needs more time.
Sessions and security logs: long enough to detect abuse (often months, not years, unless an investigation needs longer).

8.2 Reports, sites, and community content

Environmental reports may stay visible as part of the civic map for a long time or indefinitely because they document conditions in the public interest. If you exercise erasure rights, we may remove or anonymise your personal link to a report (for example detaching your account) while keeping non-personal location and imagery that cannot identify you, unless law requires otherwise. We handle erasure and anonymisation requests in line with this description.

Photos and rich media: retained with the report until deletion or anonymisation rules apply.
Comments, votes, saves: retained until you delete them, we moderate them away, or the related site is removed according to product rules.

8.3 Events

Cleanup events stay linked to sites for scheduling history; any participant lists follow the same principles as account data.

8.4 Marketing and contact history

Marketing consents and proof of opt-in or opt-out: as required by law, usually a few years after the last message.

8.5 Legal, tax, and backups

Records we must keep for accounting, tax, or litigation hold periods set by Macedonian law.
Backups rotate; purging from backups may lag production deletion by up to 90 days.

8.6 Inactive accounts

We may warn you before deleting long-inactive accounts, then remove personal data subject to the report anonymisation rules above.

sections.title: 9. Your rights under GDPR
sections.body:
Under GDPR and Macedonian data protection law, you have the following rights:

9.1 Right of access (Article 15)

You have the right to obtain:

Confirmation whether we process your personal data
A copy of your personal data
Information about processing purposes, categories, recipients, and retention periods

How to exercise: Email [PRIVACY_EMAIL] with subject "Data Access Request"
Response time: Within 30 days (may extend to 60 days for complex requests)
Cost: Free for the first request; reasonable fee for excessive requests

9.2 Right to rectification (Article 16)

You have the right to correct inaccurate or incomplete personal data.

How to exercise:

Update directly through account settings in the apps where available
Email [PRIVACY_EMAIL] with correct information
Response time: Without undue delay, maximum 30 days

9.3 Right to erasure / "Right to be forgotten" (Article 17)

You have the right to request deletion of your personal data when:

Data is no longer necessary for original purposes
You withdraw consent (where consent was the legal basis)
You object to processing and there are no overriding legitimate grounds
Data was unlawfully processed
Legal obligation requires deletion

Exceptions: We may refuse deletion when required for:

Legal compliance (e.g., tax records)
Legal claims defense
Public interest or scientific research

How to exercise: Account settings > Delete Account in the apps where available, or email [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
Response time: 30 days; data purged within 90 days including backups

9.4 Right to restriction of processing (Article 18)

You have the right to limit processing when:

You contest data accuracy (during verification)
Processing is unlawful but you oppose deletion
We no longer need data, but you need it for legal claims
You objected to processing (pending verification of legitimate grounds)

How to exercise: Email [PRIVACY_EMAIL] with specific restriction request
Effect: Data marked and stored, but not actively processed

9.5 Right to data portability (Article 20)

You have the right to receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format and transmit it to another controller.

Applies to:

Data provided by you
Processing based on consent or contract
Processing carried out by automated means

How to exercise: Account settings > Export Data in the apps where available, or email [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
Format: JSON, CSV, or PDF
Response time: 30 days

9.6 Right to object (Article 21)

9.6.1 Marketing

You have the absolute right to object to direct marketing.

How to exercise: Click "Unsubscribe" in emails, or update preferences in account settings in the apps where available

9.6.2 Legitimate interest processing

You have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interests.

How to exercise: Email [PRIVACY_EMAIL] with specific objection
Effect: We will cease processing unless we demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds

9.6.3 Profiling and automated decision-making (Article 22)

We do not currently engage in automated decision-making with legal or significant effects. If this changes, you will have the right to:

Obtain human intervention
Express your point of view
Contest the decision

9.7 Right to withdraw consent

Where processing is based on consent, you can withdraw at any time without affecting prior lawful processing.

How to exercise: Account settings > Privacy Preferences in the apps where available, or email [PRIVACY_EMAIL]

9.8 Right to lodge a complaint

You have the right to file a complaint with the supervisory authority:

Agency for Personal Data Protection
Republic of Macedonia
Address: [SUPERVISORY_AUTHORITY_ADDRESS]
Website: https://www.dzlp.mk
Email: info@dzlp.mk
Phone: [SUPERVISORY_AUTHORITY_PHONE]

For EU residents: You may also lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority in your EU member state of residence.

sections.title: 10. Data security
sections.body:
We implement technical and organizational measures appropriate to the risk, taking into account the nature of the processing and the state of the art.

10.1 Measures we may use

Depending on how the service is hosted and developed, measures may include:

Encryption of personal data in transit (for example TLS)
Passwords stored using strong one-way hashing
Access controls for systems and accounts, including multi-factor authentication for administrative access where we deploy it
Patching, monitoring, and logging for reliability and abuse detection
Safeguards against common application risks (for example input validation and protections against injection and cross-site scripting where applicable)

We review and adjust controls as the service evolves.

10.2 Organization and vendors

We maintain practices suited to our size and risk, including how we work with hosting and other processors we engage.

10.3 Personal data breaches

If we become aware of a breach that is likely to affect your rights and freedoms, we will notify the supervisory authority without undue delay and within 72 hours where the GDPR or applicable law requires it, and we will inform affected users when the law requires that too.

10.4 Your part

Use a strong unique password, enable multi-factor authentication in the apps if offered, sign out on shared devices, and contact us promptly if you suspect unauthorized access to your account.

sections.title: 11. Children's privacy
sections.body:
11.1 The Platform is not intended for children under 18 years of age.

11.2 We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 18 without parental consent.

11.3 If we become aware that we have collected data from a child under 18 without verified parental consent, we will delete that information as quickly as possible.

11.4 If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has provided personal data to us, please contact us at [PRIVACY_EMAIL].

11.5 Parents may supervise minors' use of the Platform, but minors may not create accounts independently.

sections.title: 12. Cookies and tracking technologies
sections.body:
The public website uses strictly necessary storage plus optional Vercel Web Analytics if you opt in through our banner. We do not run separate functional or marketing cookie toggles on the site today. Full detail is in the Cookie Policy at [COOKIE_POLICY_URL].

The mobile apps use on-device storage, push tokens, and map caches as described in Sections 3 and 6, not browser cookies.

sections.title: 13. Third-party links and services
sections.body:
13.1 The website may link to social profiles or partner pages. The apps load map tiles and geocoding from providers such as Carto (OpenStreetMap data) or Esri where offered.

13.2 Those services have their own privacy notices. We do not control them.

13.3 We do not embed social login in the current mobile product. If we add integrations, we will list them here.

sections.title: 14. Changes to this Privacy Policy
sections.body:
14.1 Notification of changes

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make material changes, we will notify you by:

Email notification to registered users (at least 30 days before changes take effect)
Prominent notice on the Platform
In-app notification
Updated "Last Updated" date at the top of this Policy

14.2 Your acceptance

Continued use of the Platform after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated Policy. If you do not agree with changes, you must stop using the Platform and may close your account.

14.3 Version history

We maintain a version history of this Policy. Previous versions are available upon request at [PRIVACY_EMAIL].

sections.title: 15. Specific provisions for Macedonian users
sections.body:
15.1 Language

This Privacy Policy is provided in Macedonian, English, and Albanian. In case of conflict, the Macedonian version prevails for users in the Republic of Macedonia.

15.2 Supervisory authority

The competent supervisory authority for data protection matters in Macedonia is:
Агенција за заштита на личните податоци
(Agency for Personal Data Protection)
Address: [AUTHORITY_ADDRESS]
Website: https://www.dzlp.mk
Email: info@dzlp.mk

15.3 Legal basis

Processing of personal data is conducted in accordance with:

Закон за заштита на личните податоци (Official Gazette No. 42/2020)
EU GDPR principles as adopted in Macedonian legislation

sections.title: 16. Contact us
sections.body:
For questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or your personal data:

Privacy inquiries:
Email: [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
Phone: [CONTACT_PHONE]
Address: [REGISTERED_ADDRESS]
Data protection officer (if applicable):
Email: [DPO_EMAIL]
General contact:
Email: [CONTACT_EMAIL]

Response time: We aim to respond to all privacy inquiries within 48 hours and resolve requests within 30 days.

BY USING THE PLATFORM, YOU CONFIRM THAT YOU HAVE READ THIS PRIVACY POLICY. WE PROCESS PERSONAL DATA AS DESCRIBED HEREIN, INCLUDING PROCESSING THAT DOES NOT RELY ON CONSENT WHERE THE LAW ALLOWS OTHER LEGAL BASES.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.body:
Effective date: [EFFECTIVE_DATE].

1.1 About this policy

This Cookie Policy explains how [LEGAL_ENTITY_NAME] ("Chisto.mk," "we," "us," or "our") uses cookies and similar storage on the Chisto.mk marketing website. Mobile apps follow the Privacy Policy for device storage, push tokens, and map caches.

Read this Policy together with:

• Privacy Policy: [PRIVACY_POLICY_URL]
• Terms of Use: [TERMS_URL]

1.2 Legal framework

We comply with the ePrivacy framework as transposed in Macedonia, the GDPR where it applies, the Law on Personal Data Protection (Official Gazette No. 42/2020), and the Law on Electronic Communications (Official Gazette No. 39/2014).

1.3 Consent on the website

Strictly necessary storage works without consent. Optional Vercel Web Analytics loads only if you accept analytics in our banner. You can reopen choices via Cookie settings in the footer ([COOKIE_PREFERENCES_URL]).

sections.title: 2. What are cookies?
sections.body:
2.1 Definition

Cookies are small files a site stores in your browser. Local storage is a related browser feature we use to remember your analytics choice.

2.2 Duration and party

• Session cookies expire when you close the browser.
• Persistent cookies or storage entries stay until they expire or you clear site data.
• First-party entries are set by our site. Third-party scripts (Vercel Analytics, when enabled) may set their own identifiers under Vercel's policies.

2.3 Mobile apps

Apps use secure storage, SQLite or file caches, Firebase Cloud Messaging tokens, and similar tools described in the Privacy Policy rather than browser cookies.

sections.title: 3. Why we use cookies
sections.body:
On the website we use storage to:

• Remember whether you accepted or rejected optional analytics
• Keep the site working (security, load balancing, and similar basics handled by our host)
• If you opt in, measure aggregate traffic with Vercel Web Analytics

We do not use marketing or social advertising pixels on this site today.

sections.title: 4. Categories we use on the website
sections.body:
4.1 Strictly necessary storage

We store your analytics choice in browser localStorage (key chisto_cookie_consent_v1) so the banner does not reset every visit and so we can respect rejections. Our hosting provider may also set short-lived technical cookies for security or delivery. Legal basis: legitimate interests and, where applicable, provision of the service you request.

4.2 Optional analytics (consent)

If you opt in, we load Vercel Web Analytics to collect aggregate page views and performance metrics. Legal basis: consent. You can withdraw consent anytime via Cookie settings ([COOKIE_PREFERENCES_URL]).

4.3 What we do not use here

We do not operate a separate marketing pixel, Facebook Pixel, or Google Ads tags on this marketing site in the current build. If that changes, we will update this Policy and the banner before enabling them.

sections.title: 5. Mobile applications
sections.body:
5.1 Technologies

The apps store login tokens securely, cache map tiles for performance, keep preferences locally, and register Firebase Cloud Messaging tokens when you enable notifications.

5.2 Advertising identifiers

We do not use the advertising ID to serve third-party ads in the current product. OS-level ad settings still apply generally on your device.

5.3 Permissions

We request access to location, camera, photos, and notifications only when a feature needs them. You can change permissions in device settings.

sections.title: 6. How to manage cookies
sections.body:
6.1 Banner and footer

On your first visit we ask whether to enable optional analytics. You can reopen the choice from "Cookie settings" in the footer ([COOKIE_PREFERENCES_URL]). The marketing site does not expose account-level cookie menus.

6.2 Browser controls

You may clear or block cookies and site data in your browser; blocking everything can break the site.

6.3 Do Not Track

Browsers may send DNT signals; we do not treat DNT as a binding legal request today because industry practice is inconsistent.

sections.title: 7. Cookie lifespan
sections.body: Consent records in localStorage remain until you clear site data or we bump the storage version. Vercel Analytics identifiers follow Vercel's documentation (often well under two years). Hosting cookies are typically short session cookies.

sections.title: 8. Social links
sections.body: The website may show ordinary links to social networks. Simply visiting our pages does not embed social login. If you click through to a network, that network's policy applies.

sections.title: 9. Data protection and security
sections.body:
9.1 Security

We serve the site over HTTPS and limit access to analytics dashboards.

9.2 Retention

Analytics data is handled under Vercel's terms. When you withdraw consent we stop loading their script on future page loads.

9.3 Transfers

Vercel may process data in the United States or other regions. We rely on appropriate safeguards described in their documentation and our Privacy Policy.

sections.title: 10. Your rights
sections.body:
Under the GDPR and applicable local law, you have rights regarding data collected through cookies, including:

• Right of access: Information about cookie-related data we hold
• Right to erasure: Deletion of cookie data where applicable
• Right to object: Object to processing based on legitimate interests where provided by law
• Right to withdraw consent: At any time for non-essential cookies
• Right to lodge a complaint: With a supervisory authority

How to exercise your rights:

• Email: [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
• Cookie settings: [COOKIE_PREFERENCES_URL]

Supervisory authority (Macedonia):

Agency for Personal Data Protection. Website: https://www.dzlp.mk. Email: info@dzlp.mk

sections.title: 11. Children's privacy
sections.body:
11.1 Our Platform is not directed at children under 18 years of age.

11.2 We do not knowingly collect cookie data from children under 18.

11.3 If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has been tracked through cookies without appropriate consent, contact us at [PRIVACY_EMAIL].

sections.title: 12. Updates to this policy
sections.body:
12.1 Policy changes

We may update this Cookie Policy to reflect changes in our use of cookies, new legal requirements, improvements to the Platform, or user feedback.

12.2 Notification

We may notify you of material changes by updating the "Last updated" date at the top, email (for significant changes where appropriate), a prominent notice on the Platform, or an updated cookie consent banner.

12.3 Your acceptance

Continued use of the Platform after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated Policy. If you do not agree, adjust your cookie settings or stop using the Platform.

sections.title: 13. Contact information
sections.body:
Questions about cookies:

• Email: [PRIVACY_EMAIL]
• Phone: [CONTACT_PHONE]
• Address: [REGISTERED_ADDRESS]

Data protection officer (if applicable):

• Email: [DPO_EMAIL]

General contact:

• Email: [CONTACT_EMAIL]

dataPage.title: Your data and account
dataPage.lead: This is Chisto.mk's public account deletion and data rights page. It explains how to request deletion of your account and associated personal data, export your data, correct mistakes, object or restrict processing where applicable, and contact us about privacy questions.
dataPage.requestIntro: Use the channel below to request account deletion, report anonymisation where applicable, a copy or export of your personal data, corrections, restriction, objection where applicable, or other privacy support. Email requests go to [PRIVACY_EMAIL]. We may ask for reasonable proof of identity, such as an email from your registered address or a one-time verification step, before acting on sensitive requests.
dataPage.requestMissing: The data request channel is temporarily unavailable. Email us at [PRIVACY_EMAIL].

sections.title: 1. Account and profile
sections.body:
Accounts are created in the Chisto.mk mobile apps with your email, mobile phone number, password, and phone verification using one-time codes. Keep credentials secret.

Update profile fields inside the app where offered. Use a strong unique password. If the app offers authenticator-based MFA, turning it on reduces takeover risk.

The public website may not provide full registration; use the apps for account features.

sections.title: 2. Viewing and exporting your data
sections.body:
You may have the right to obtain confirmation of processing and a copy of your personal data (GDPR Art. 15). Where technically feasible, we provide self-service views in the product; otherwise submit a request via the channel above.

For data portability (GDPR Art. 20), you may request a structured, commonly used, machine-readable export (for example JSON or CSV) of data you provided and that we process by automated means based on contract or consent. We will provide it within a reasonable period, typically within thirty (30) days of verification unless applicable law requires a shorter or longer deadline.

sections.title: 3. Corrections
sections.body: If personal data is inaccurate or incomplete, you may request rectification (GDPR Art. 16) using the same channel. We will correct obvious errors promptly; complex disputes may require additional verification.

sections.title: 4. Account deletion and what we keep
sections.body:
You may request deletion of your Account (GDPR Art. 17) via the data request channel or in-app settings if available. We review verified deletion requests within 30 days. After approval, we remove or anonymise personal data in production systems except where retention is necessary: for example to comply with accounting or tax law, to establish or defend legal claims during applicable limitation periods, to prevent abuse, or to meet a specific legal obligation. Backups and security archives may take up to 90 days to expire or be overwritten. We will explain any limited retention if it applies to you.

Some environmental report content you published may remain visible without personal identifiers, because civic environmental observations can document conditions in the public interest. Where possible, we detach your account or other personal identifiers while keeping non-personal report facts.

sections.title: 5. Marketing and communications preferences
sections.body:
Service-related messages (security, reports, events) may be necessary.

Newsletters or marketing need a lawful basis, usually consent. Unsubscribe with the link in each email or contact us. Website contact and update notification emails are delivered through Resend today. Mailchimp will be used only if we later enable a newsletter list, and this page and the Privacy Policy will be updated before that happens.

sections.title: 6. Cookies and similar technologies
sections.body: On the website, use Cookie settings in the footer to allow or refuse optional Vercel Web Analytics. Strictly necessary storage still applies. See the Cookie Policy for detail.

sections.title: 7. Response times, appeals, and complaints
sections.body:
We aim to respond to verified requests without undue delay and within any statutory deadline (for example one month under the GDPR, with possible extensions for complex requests).

If you are unhappy with our response, you may complain to [SUPERVISORY_AUTHORITY_NAME] or your local data protection authority. You may also seek judicial remedy where applicable law allows.

getting-started.cardSummary: Install the app from the App Store or Google Play, sign in, pass the Macedonia location gate, and finish the coach tour.
getting-started.title: Set up Chisto.mk

blocks.text: Chisto.mk is available for iOS and Android from the App Store and Google Play. You can also use the download buttons on the website home page.

blocks.title: Before you install
items: Search the store for «Chisto.mk» and verify the publisher matches our official listing.
Use Wi-Fi if your data plan is limited; the first launch may download map assets.
Update iOS or Android when prompted so the camera, maps, and security patches stay current.

blocks.text: If the store page is unavailable in your region, open the website on a desktop browser and follow the official store links from there.

Open the app and follow the sign-up flow. You will use a phone number you can verify with SMS (or a similar one-time code), an email address, your name, and a password that meets the app rules. This matches the pattern described in our Terms of use.

blocks.title: What to prepare
items: A phone number you can access for the verification code.
An email inbox you check regularly for account notices.
A strong password that you do not reuse on other services.

blocks.text: Keep your device clock accurate. Large clock skew can sometimes interfere with code-based sign-in.
Chisto.mk is available in Macedonia. During sign up the app checks that your device location is inside the country. After that you land on the Home feed with nearby pollution sites and feed filters.

blocks.title: First steps on Home feed
items: Scroll the feed or tap feed filters such as All, Urgent, Nearby, and Top support.
Tap a site card to open photos, history, and Take action options.
Open the Map tab when you want pins and the heatmap view.

blocks.text: If you travel outside Macedonia, browsing may still work, but reporting and eco actions require you to be in the country.
After your first sign in, a coach tour walks you through the main tabs: Home feed, Map tab, Reports list, Report FAB, Events, and your profile avatar.

blocks.title: Main tabs
items: Home feed shows ranked site cards and filters.
Map tab shows pins, heatmap, and site detail sheets.
Reports lists your submissions with status chips.
Events lists cleanup and eco-action listings.
The Report FAB in the center starts the 4-step report wizard.

blocks.text: You can skip the coach tour anytime. Reopen it later from profile settings if you want a refresher.

You control whether the app may send push notifications in your device settings and in the app where offered. Our Privacy Policy explains how personal data is processed when you report issues or join events.

blocks.title: Where to look
items: iOS: Settings → Notifications → Chisto.mk.
Android: Settings → Apps → Chisto.mk → Notifications.
In-app toggles may exist for specific categories such as events or report status.

sign-in-and-verification.cardSummary: Sign in with phone and password, complete SMS OTP verification, and recover access when codes stop arriving.
sign-in-and-verification.title: Sign in and verification

blocks.text: Returning users sign in with the same phone number and password created at registration. The app formats Macedonian numbers automatically.

blocks.title: Sign in steps
items: Open Chisto.mk and tap Sign in.
Enter your phone number and password.
Tap Sign in and wait for the session to load.

blocks.text: Keep your device clock set to automatic. Large clock skew can break SMS code validation.
New accounts and some security flows send a one-time code by SMS. Enter the code before it expires. Codes are short lived and cannot be reused.

blocks.title: If the code does not arrive
items: Confirm airplane mode is off and you have signal.
Wait one minute, then tap Resend code.
Check that the phone number has no typos.
Disable SMS filtering apps that might hide sender messages.

blocks.text: Paste the code carefully. Autofill from Messages usually works on iOS; Android SMS autofill may also insert the code.
Registration requires your device location to be inside Macedonia. Sign in itself does not repeat the gate every time, but reporting, eco actions, and some edits check location again.

blocks.title: When location is blocked
items: Enable Location Services for Chisto.mk while using the app.
Turn off mock location or developer GPS tools.
Move outdoors briefly so GPS can settle near tall buildings.

blocks.text: Use Forgot password on the sign in screen. You receive instructions by email or SMS depending on the flow active in your build.

blocks.title: Recovery checklist
items: Use the same email you registered with.
Open recovery links on the same device when possible.
Choose a new password that meets the app rules.

blocks.text: Sign out from profile settings before handing your phone to someone else. Draft reports stay on the device until you discard them or submit.

blocks.title: Good habits
items: Enable a screen lock on phones you carry daily.
Do not share SMS codes with anyone.
Update the app from the App Store or Google Play when updates appear.

app-permissions.cardSummary: Allow camera, photos, location, notifications, and motion access so reporting, map pins, and event check-in work reliably.
app-permissions.title: App permissions

blocks.text: Chisto.mk asks for device permissions only when a feature needs them. Denying access blocks parts of the 4-step report wizard, Map tab accuracy, or event QR check-in.

blocks.title: Core permissions
items: Camera and Photos for Evidence stage photos.
Location while using the app for pins in Macedonia.
Notifications for report status and event updates.

blocks.text: You can change permissions later in device Settings. The app shows inline hints when something is missing.
Choose While Using the App. The Location stage needs a pin inside Macedonia. Nearby feed filters and map centering also use your position.

blocks.title: Fix location issues
items: iOS: Settings, Privacy and Security, Location Services, Chisto.mk.
Android: Settings, Location, App permissions, Chisto.mk, Allow while using.
Enable Precise Location on iOS when offered.
Restart the app after changing the setting.

blocks.text: The Evidence stage accepts up to five photos from the camera or library. Without access, you cannot complete a report.

blocks.title: Recommended settings
items: Allow camera access when prompted during your first report.
Allow photo library access to attach existing images.
Free storage space if uploads fail repeatedly.

blocks.text: Capture in daylight when safe. You can add library photos later in the same draft.
Push alerts cover report status, nearby reports, cleanup events, and event chat. You can fine tune eight notification groups inside the app even when push is allowed.

blocks.title: Enable alerts
items: Tap Allow when your device asks during onboarding.
iOS: Settings, Notifications, Chisto.mk.
Android: Settings, Apps, Chisto.mk, Notifications.
Open the in app inbox preferences to mute or snooze groups.

blocks.text: Open device Settings for Chisto.mk and review each toggle. Motion or fitness data is not required for core reporting.

blocks.title: After you change settings
items: Force close and reopen Chisto.mk once.
Retry the action that failed, such as Report FAB or QR scan.
Check troubleshooting if errors persist.

offline-and-slow-networks.cardSummary: Save report drafts locally, queue uploads on weak signal, and avoid duplicate submissions while pending.
offline-and-slow-networks.title: Offline and slow networks

sections.title: How the app uses the network

blocks.text: Maps, photos, and submissions sync when connectivity returns. Some map tiles cache so you can browse lightly offline. Account actions usually need a live session.

blocks.title: Expectations
items: Draft fields save locally until you submit.
Large photos take longer on mobile data.
Event chat and check-in need a connection.

blocks.text: Pending uploads are normal briefly after switching from Wi-Fi to mobile data.
The 4-step report wizard saves progress as a draft. You can leave mid flow and resume from the Reports tab or the Report FAB sheet.

blocks.title: Draft tips
items: Finish Evidence and Details before you lose signal if possible.
Confirm the Location pin when you regain GPS.
Submit on Wi-Fi when photos are large.

blocks.text: Pending means the device is still sending data or the server has not confirmed receipt. Do not start a second report for the same issue while pending spins.

blocks.title: If you are stuck
items: Open the report summary and look for retry prompts.
Capture smaller photos if uploads fail repeatedly.
Wait on stable Wi-Fi before force closing the app.

blocks.text: Report credits are consumed on successful submit, not while a draft is still local.
International roaming can block background uploads. Finish uploads before flights when possible.

blocks.title: Travel tips
items: Reconnect to Wi-Fi before editing long descriptions.
Double check event reminders if time zones shift.
QR check-in needs live data at the venue.

blocks.text: Toggle cellular data for Chisto.mk in device Settings if roaming blocks only this app.
If the app crashes twice while uploading, pause and collect OS version and app version before contacting support. Reinstalling can clear local drafts.

blocks.title: Before you write to us
items: Restart the device once.
Confirm you are on the latest build from the App Store or Google Play.
Capture screenshots of error text and blur personal details.

troubleshooting.cardSummary: Fix sign-in, SMS codes, map location, camera uploads, and know when to update or contact support.
troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting common issues

blocks.text: Most issues are fixed with connectivity checks, permissions, and the latest app version.

blocks.title: Try this first
items: Toggle airplane mode off and on.
Confirm date and time automatic.
Restart the app after changing permissions.

blocks.text: If a crash repeats, note your app version and device model before contacting support.
Most issues are fixed with connectivity checks, permissions, and the latest app version.

blocks.title: Try this first
items: Toggle airplane mode off and on.
Confirm date and time automatic.
Restart the app after changing permissions.

blocks.text: If a crash repeats, note your app version and device model before contacting support.
Most issues are fixed with connectivity checks, permissions, and the latest app version.

blocks.title: Try this first
items: Toggle airplane mode off and on.
Confirm date and time automatic.
Restart the app after changing permissions.

blocks.text: If a crash repeats, note your app version and device model before contacting support.
You receive a limited pool of report credits. When all credits and the emergency allowance are used, the Report FAB shows a cooldown message.

blocks.title: Unlock more reports
items: Join eco-action and verify attendance at check-in.
Create an eco action as an organizer after passing the organizer quiz.
Wait for the emergency unlock timer if shown.

blocks.text: Most issues are fixed with connectivity checks, permissions, and the latest app version.

blocks.title: Try this first
items: Toggle airplane mode off and on.
Confirm date and time automatic.
Restart the app after changing permissions.

partnerships-for-organisations.cardSummary: How municipalities, NGOs, schools, and journalists can use Chisto.mk map data and events responsibly.
partnerships-for-organisations.title: Partnerships for municipalities, NGOs, and media

blocks.text: This article is for organisations that want to use public map data, coordinate cleanups, or explore collaboration. It is not legal advice.

blocks.title: Typical readers
items: Municipal environment desks planning field follow up.
NGOs mapping volunteer capacity near reported sites.
Schools hosting supervised eco actions.
Journalists verifying locations before publishing.

blocks.text: Student groups should work with a teacher or NGO contact before publishing events.
Residents file reports through the mobile app. Approved reports become public pollution sites on the map. Organizers can publish cleanup events and eco actions subject to moderation.

blocks.title: Public signals you can rely on
items: Site pins with photos and community history.
Event listings with RSVP and check-in data when organizers enable it.
Status labels such as Under review, Approved, and Declined on submissions.

blocks.text: Map pins are civic signals, not automatic legal findings. Use official channels for regulated enforcement.
Gather your goal, geography, and timeline. If you need bulk data, explain public benefit and how you will protect personal data.

blocks.title: Helpful attachments
items: Official email from an organisational domain when possible.
One paragraph on intended use of map or event information.
Preferred language: Macedonian, Albanian, or English.

blocks.text: Binding rules live in our Terms, Privacy policy, and data pages. The help centre explains product behavior for residents.

blocks.title: Where to read more
items: Use the data page for retention and rights paths.
Use the privacy page before processing identifiable information.
Link staff to the help hub so field teams share app vocabulary.

home-feed-and-sites.cardSummary: Browse the Home feed, use feed filters, open site cards, and start Take action flows from a pollution site.
home-feed-and-sites.title: Home feed and pollution sites

blocks.text: The Home feed is the default tab after sign in. It shows pollution sites ranked for your area with photos, status, and community activity.

blocks.title: What you see
items: Site cards with headline, location hint, and support counts.
Badges when a site needs attention or has new reports.
Your profile avatar in the header for quick access to settings.

blocks.text: Pull to refresh when you return from the field with new photos.
Tap feed filters above the list to change ranking. Filters include All, Urgent, Nearby, and Top support.

blocks.title: When to use each filter
items: All: balanced default ranking.
Urgent: new or unverified sites that need community attention.
Nearby: closest reports around your current location.
Top support: sites with the most community upvotes.

blocks.text: Nearby requires location permission. Enable it in Settings if the filter looks empty.
Tap a card to open the site detail sheet with photos, timeline, comments, and Take action options.

blocks.title: On the site page
items: Swipe photos and read the latest approved report text.
View history events such as new reports and cleanups.
Upvote or comment when you want to show support.

blocks.text: Take action opens options such as filing a new report, Join eco-action, or viewing linked cleanup events.

blocks.title: Common choices
items: Report again when you have fresh Evidence photos.
Join eco-action to RSVP to an existing cleanup.
Create eco action when you plan to host a new event.

blocks.text: Switch to the Map tab to see the same sites as pins. Toggle the heatmap to spot clusters of activity.

blocks.title: Map tab tips
items: Pinch to zoom until street names match the ground.
Tap a pin to open the same site sheet as the feed.
Use Recenter when you want to jump back to your position.

exploring-the-map.cardSummary: Navigate pins, toggle the heatmap, open site sheets, and decide when to file a new report.
exploring-the-map.title: Exploring the Map tab

blocks.text: The Map tab is a shared civic view of pollution sites in Macedonia. Each pin is a place where someone documented an environmental concern.

blocks.title: Quick orientation
items: Zoom until street names and landmarks match what you see outside.
Tap a pin to open photos, descriptions, and history.
Counts change as moderators approve new reports.

blocks.text: Toggle Show heatmap to highlight areas with more reported activity. Hide heatmap when you need a cleaner view for precise pin placement.

blocks.title: Using the heatmap
items: Look for hot zones when planning group cleanups.
Turn heatmap off before confirming a Location pin.
Combine with Nearby feed filter when scouting on foot.

blocks.text: Heatmap density reflects reported sites, not official enforcement priority.
A site sheet summarises community submissions. It supports transparency, not assigning blame to individuals.

blocks.title: What you can rely on
items: Photos and text come from contributors and moderators.
Timestamps show recency when displayed.
Take action links reporting, events, and comments in one place.

blocks.text: Do not treat map pins as legal proof on their own.
Use the map while stationary or as a passenger. Keep awareness of traffic and private property boundaries.

blocks.title: Good habits
items: Prefer daylight for first visits to unfamiliar edges.
Step back and capture from a distance if a path feels unsafe.
Respect no entry signs even when rubbish is visible beyond them.

blocks.text: Do not use the Map tab while driving. Pull over safely first.
Open the Report FAB when you can add fresh evidence or correct a pin. Duplicates without new facts slow moderation.

blocks.title: Before you start
items: Gather photos and note what changed since the last visit.
Confirm you have report credits available.
Complete all four stages: Evidence, Details, Location, Review.

verifying-sites-in-the-field.cardSummary: Plan a safe visit, compare the pin to what you see, and file a new report when evidence clearly changed.
verifying-sites-in-the-field.title: Verifying pollution sites on the ground

blocks.text: Open the site from Home feed or Map tab, note the pin and photos, then plan daylight access from a public vantage.

blocks.title: Checklist
items: Charge your phone and expect weak signal in some valleys.
Tell a colleague where you go if you work alone.
Avoid private land unless you already have permission.

blocks.text: Stand where the map suggests and scan for the same cues in photos: kerb lines, trees, buildings, or shoreline bends.

blocks.title: When to report again
items: The hazard moved materially since the last photos.
The pin sits on the wrong block or riverbank.
You can document a risk that was not visible earlier.

blocks.text: Small GPS offsets happen near cliffs and tall buildings. Large mismatches deserve a careful new report.
Use the Report FAB and complete Evidence with up to five photos. Add Details that explain what changed since prior visits.

blocks.title: Field photo tips
items: Start wide for context, then move closer to the problem.
Include scale cues when size is unclear.
Stay out of traffic, unstable ground, and unknown liquids.

blocks.text: On the Location stage, drag the pin to the exact spot inside Macedonia. Tap Confirm location before Review.

blocks.title: Pin quality
items: GPS helps, but confirm visually on the map.
Describe access limits in Details when paths are blocked.
Submit only when Review shows all requirements met.

blocks.text: If GPS is outside Macedonia, drag the pin manually before you confirm.
Do not enter unstable piles, fast water, or industrial yards without training. Treat residents courteously.
If you discover immediate danger to life, use official emergency channels first, then document when safe.

report-a-site.cardSummary: Use the Report FAB and complete Evidence, Details, Location, and Review to submit a report that moderators can verify.
report-a-site.title: Report a pollution site

blocks.text: Tap the Report FAB from any main tab. If you have a saved draft, choose Continue draft or start fresh. Confirm you have report credits before you begin.

blocks.title: Before you start
items: Stay safe around traffic, unstable ground, and unknown liquids.
Check report credits on your profile if the FAB shows a cooldown.
Allow camera and location permissions when prompted.

blocks.text: Reporting requires your location to be in Macedonia.
Add up to five photos that show the polluted area clearly. At least one photo is required to continue.

blocks.title: What to capture
items: Wide shot for context, then closer frames of waste, stains, or debris.
Daylight and a steady hand help moderators verify faster.
Tap the stage help icon for examples any time.

blocks.text: Never enter private property without permission. Capture from a public vantage when possible.
Choose a category and write a short headline anyone could understand. Add optional severity, description, and cleanup effort notes.

blocks.title: Required fields
items: Category that matches what you saw.
Headline like a news title, not an essay.
Optional description for access, smell, color of water, or landmarks.

blocks.text: Only category and title are required to reach the Location stage.
Move the pin to the exact pollution site inside Macedonia. Zoom until buildings or shoreline match what you see on the ground, then tap Confirm location.

blocks.title: Pin tips
items: Drag the pin if GPS is offset near tall buildings.
The map cannot pan outside Macedonia.
Return to this stage from Review if the pin needs a fix.

blocks.text: Double-tap Recenter to jump to your GPS position when you are inside Macedonia.
Review summarizes photos, Details, and the pin. Submit sends the report for moderation. Status starts as Under review until moderators approve or decline it.

blocks.title: After submit
items: Track status in the Reports tab.
Approved reports link to a public pollution site.
Declined reports include a reason when moderators provide one.

report-statuses-and-drafts.cardSummary: Understand Under review, Approved, and Declined labels, and resume saved drafts from Reports or the Report FAB.
report-statuses-and-drafts.title: Report statuses and drafts

blocks.text: Each submission moves through moderation before it affects the public map. Status chips appear on the Reports tab and in notifications.

blocks.title: Common statuses
items: Under review: moderators are checking evidence and location.
Approved: the report linked to a public pollution site.
Declined: the submission did not meet guidelines.

blocks.text: Already reported may appear when your submission duplicates an existing open site.
Open the Reports tab to search and filter your submissions. Tap a row for the full timeline and linked site.

blocks.title: Reports tab actions
items: Filter by status such as Under review or Approved.
Open approved reports to see the public site page.
Read moderator notes on declined items when available.

blocks.text: Pull to refresh the Reports tab after you receive a Report status notification.
Leaving the wizard saves a draft locally with your current stage. Draft chips show photo count on the Reports list.

blocks.title: Resume a draft
items: Tap the draft row in Reports.
Or tap Report FAB and choose Continue draft.
Discard draft from the resume sheet if you want to start over.

blocks.text: Drafts survive app restarts but may be lost if you reinstall or clear app data.
Weak networks may leave a submit pending. Wait on Wi-Fi before you discard or duplicate the same report.

blocks.title: If submit fails
items: Reopen the draft and tap Submit again on Review.
Check troubleshooting for camera or upload errors.
Contact support if the same error repeats after an update.

blocks.text: Successful submits use report credits. Join eco-action or create an eco action to unlock more when you hit the limit.

blocks.title: See your balance
items: Open profile and find the Report credits card.
Read the cooldown message when all credits are spent.
Verify event attendance to restore capacity faster.

join-a-cleanup-event.cardSummary: Browse eco actions on the Events tab, RSVP with Join eco-action, and prepare for QR check-in on the day.
join-a-cleanup-event.title: Join a cleanup event

blocks.text: Browse upcoming cleanups in the app, read the organiser notes, and join when registration is open.

blocks.title: Apple-style checklist
items: Check meeting point, duration, and difficulty before you travel.
Bring gloves or bags only if the listing asks for them.
Update your RSVP if plans change so headcounts stay accurate.

blocks.text: Tap Join eco-action on an open listing to RSVP. You must be in Macedonia for join flows that create commitments on the map.

blocks.title: After you join
items: Add the event to your calendar once you commit.
Enable Cleanup events notifications for last minute changes.
Cancel promptly if you cannot attend so headcounts stay accurate.

blocks.text: Joining and verifying attendance can unlock additional report credits.

sections.title: From a site Take action sheet

blocks.text: On a pollution site, Take action may list linked eco actions near that pin. This helps you join cleanups where community reports already exist.

blocks.title: Site linked events
items: Open the site from Home feed or Map tab.
Tap Take action and pick Join eco-action when listed.
Review the pin matches the site you care about.

blocks.text: Browse upcoming cleanups in the app, read the organiser notes, and join when registration is open.

blocks.title: Apple-style checklist
items: Check meeting point, duration, and difficulty before you travel.
Bring gloves or bags only if the listing asks for them.
Update your RSVP if plans change so headcounts stay accurate.

blocks.text: Most events use QR check-in at arrival. Open the event detail on the day and follow Event check-in and chat for scan steps and chat rules.

blocks.title: Day of reminders
items: Arrive with battery and mobile data or venue Wi-Fi.
Open the event screen before you queue at the organiser desk.
Keep Event chat notifications on for venue updates.

event-check-in-and-chat.cardSummary: Scan the organiser QR at arrival, confirm attendance, and use event chat for venue updates.
event-check-in-and-chat.title: Event check-in and chat

blocks.text: Open the event you joined from the Events tab. Confirm the meeting pin, start time, and any organiser messages in event chat.

blocks.title: Prepare your phone
items: Charge above 30 percent before you travel.
Allow camera access for QR scanning.
Enable Event chat notifications for route changes.

blocks.text: At the venue the organiser displays a QR code. Tap Check in on the event screen and scan the newest code shown.

blocks.title: Scan steps
items: Open the joined event detail.
Tap Check in or Scan QR.
Point the camera at the organiser screen, not a photo shared earlier.
Wait for the success confirmation before you walk away.

blocks.text: QR codes refresh automatically. Scan the live code on the organiser device.
Verified attendance can unlock report credits and points. If scan fails, ask the organiser to refresh the QR session.

blocks.title: Common scan errors
items: Wrong event QR: confirm you opened the correct listing.
QR expired: ask the organiser to show a fresh code.
Already used: wait while the organiser regenerates the session.

blocks.text: Stand in good light and hold the phone steady a forearm length from the screen.
Event chat lets organisers and volunteers coordinate before, during, and after the shift. Messages notify through the Event chat preference group.

blocks.title: Chat etiquette
items: Keep messages short and on topic for the venue.
Do not share personal phone numbers publicly in chat.
Report abusive messages through trust and safety tools.

blocks.text: Follow organiser instructions for zones, waste bags, and photo rules. After the event, upload after photos when the flow invites them.

blocks.title: Wrap up
items: Thank hosts through event chat when appropriate.
Avoid faces or licence plates unless organisers allow them.
Leave the listing a fair rating if the app prompts you.

hosting-a-cleanup-event.cardSummary: Pass the organizer quiz, publish an eco action, run QR check-in, and moderate event chat on the day.
hosting-a-cleanup-event.title: Host a cleanup event

blocks.text: First time hosts complete the organizer quiz before Create eco action unlocks. Questions cover safety, moderation, and responsible use of the platform.

blocks.title: Quiz tips
items: Read each question carefully. Sessions expire if you pause too long.
Reload the quiz if you see a session error message.
Certification stays on your profile after you pass.

blocks.text: You must be in Macedonia to create an eco action.
From Events or Take action on a site, tap Create eco action. Fill required fields such as title, meeting pin, schedule, and capacity.

blocks.title: Before you publish
items: Confirm land access with owners or municipalities when required.
Set realistic difficulty and kit expectations.
Link the event to a pollution site when the cleanup targets a report.

blocks.text: New listings may show Under review until moderators approve them.
Events can be Under review, Approved, or Declined. Edit and resubmit when moderators ask for changes.

blocks.title: Dashboard labels
items: Under review: volunteers cannot join yet.
Approved: Join eco-action opens for everyone.
Declined: read the banner and tap Edit and resubmit.

blocks.text: Open the organiser dashboard and display the live QR for volunteers. Invalidate previous QR codes if a screenshot leaked.

blocks.title: On site leadership
items: Brief everyone on hazards, buddy pairs, and first aid.
Refresh QR before each wave if codes expire.
Use event chat for short updates instead of long threads.

blocks.text: Close the loop with thank you notes, after photos, and honest feedback. Organizer approved events can earn profile points.

blocks.title: Wrap up
items: Mark the event complete when the shift ends.
Log municipal follow ups separately from volunteer chat.
Archive chat decisions so future hosts learn from your runbook.

your-profile-and-settings.cardSummary: Edit your avatar, language, home location, notification prefs, and account controls from your profile.
your-profile-and-settings.title: Your profile and settings

blocks.text: Tap your avatar from Home feed or any main tab. Profile shows points, weekly rankings, report credits, and activity summaries.

blocks.title: Profile sections
items: Points and level card with recent reasons.
Report credits balance and cooldown hints.
Links to settings, notifications, and help.

blocks.text: Update display name, avatar photo, and bio from profile edit. Keep information respectful and avoid sharing private contact details publicly.

blocks.title: Edit steps
items: Tap Edit profile on your profile screen.
Choose a square photo that reads well at small size.
Save and confirm the preview looks correct.

blocks.text: Use a neutral photo if you represent an organisation publicly.
Switch interface language among system default, English, Macedonian, and Albanian. Set home location to tune feed ranking when you are away from GPS.

blocks.title: Location settings
items: Home location must be inside Macedonia.
Update it when you move to a new city for daily browsing.
Map pins for reports still use the Location stage GPS pin.

blocks.text: Settings holds password change, notification shortcuts, legal links, and sign out. Sign out before sharing your device.

blocks.title: Common tasks
items: Open notification preferences from settings or the inbox.
Replay the coach tour if you skipped onboarding.
Review Terms and Privacy links before you invite minors.

blocks.text: Block user from another profile or from an abusive comment thread. Blocked people cannot interact with you in app social features.

blocks.title: When to block
items: Use block for harassment or spam directed at you.
Blocking is reversible from settings when you choose.
Report serious threats through Contact as well.

points-rankings-and-credits.cardSummary: Earn points for approved reports and events, climb weekly rankings, and manage report credits.
points-rankings-and-credits.title: Points, rankings, and report credits

blocks.text: Points reward constructive participation. Common reasons include report approved, eco action approved, and verified event attendance.

blocks.title: Typical point events
items: Report approved after moderation.
Eco action approved for organizers.
Verified check-in at a cleanup you joined.

blocks.text: Points are gamification only. They do not replace official environmental enforcement.
Profile shows weekly rankings for reports, eco actions, and related activity. Rankings reset on a schedule shown in the app.

blocks.title: Ranking tips
items: Quality reports beat rushed duplicates.
Join eco-action and check in to boost event participation.
Compare your rank with friends to stay motivated.

blocks.text: Weekly rankings subtitle in profile mentions reports, eco actions, and more.
Report credits limit how many standard reports you can submit in a period. The profile card shows remaining credits and retry timers.

blocks.title: When credits run out
items: Read the cooldown modal on the Report FAB.
Join eco-action and verify QR attendance to unlock more.
Create an eco action after organizer quiz certification.

blocks.text: Emergency allowance may appear once per cycle. Watch the retry timer in the modal.
Levels group your lifetime points. System notifications may announce achievements or milestones.

blocks.title: Stay informed
items: Leave the System notification group enabled for achievements.
Open the points card to read recent reasons.
Share wins in event chat, not public phone numbers.

blocks.text: Moderators decline gaming behavior such as duplicate spam or fake check-ins. Play fair so rankings stay meaningful for everyone.

blocks.title: Avoid
items: Mass low quality reports to farm points.
Sharing QR screenshots to fake attendance.
Harassing users who rank above you.

account-and-data.cardSummary: Protect your login, understand what we store, and use the right pages for privacy and data requests.
account-and-data.title: Your account, privacy, and data requests

blocks.text: Understand what the account stores, how to stay safe on devices, and how to exercise your rights in Macedonia.

blocks.title: Key points
items: Use unique passwords and enable OS updates.
Export or delete requests may need identity checks.
Legal pages on this site carry binding wording when in doubt.

blocks.text: Understand what the account stores, how to stay safe on devices, and how to exercise your rights in Macedonia.

blocks.title: Key points
items: Use unique passwords and enable OS updates.
Export or delete requests may need identity checks.
Legal pages on this site carry binding wording when in doubt.

items: Use unique passwords and enable OS updates.
Export or delete requests may need identity checks.
Legal pages on this site carry binding wording when in doubt.

blocks.title: Key points
items: Use unique passwords and enable OS updates.
Export or delete requests may need identity checks.
Legal pages on this site carry binding wording when in doubt.

notifications-in-the-app.cardSummary: Manage the inbox, eight notification groups, snooze controls, and OS push settings together.
notifications-in-the-app.title: Notifications in the app

blocks.text: Tap the bell icon to open the notifications inbox. Similar alerts collapse into expandable notification groups by day.

blocks.title: Inbox actions
items: Tap a row to open the related site or event.
Expand a group to read every message in the stack.
Mark a group as read or archive it when done.

blocks.text: Event chat stacks by conversation so one tap opens the full thread.
Preferences organize alerts into eight groups you can mute or snooze independently.

blocks.title: The eight groups
items: Site updates
Report status
Upvotes
Comments
Nearby reports
Cleanup events
Event chat
System, including achievements and welcome messages

blocks.text: Snooze Event chat the night before a cleanup if you need sleep, then re-enable it on event morning.
Notifications keep you informed about reports and events without overwhelming you.

items: OS settings override in-app toggles.

trust-safety-and-moderation.title: Trust, safety, and moderation

blocks.text: Chisto.mk publishes civic environmental signals. Moderation reduces spam, protects people from harassment, and keeps the map readable for partners in Macedonia.

blocks.title: What reviewers look for
items: Evidence that matches the Location pin and time window.
Respectful language without personal attacks.
Duplicates folded into the same site when that keeps the story clearer.

blocks.text: Reports and eco actions show Under review until moderators decide. Approved content appears publicly. Declined content includes guidance when moderators provide it.

blocks.title: Status meanings
items: Under review: human review in progress.
Approved: visible on Home feed, Map tab, or Events.
Declined: does not meet guidelines. Edit and resubmit when allowed.

blocks.text: Assume good intent from neighbours and organisers. Focus on conditions you can photograph or describe factually.

blocks.title: Community norms
items: No doxxing: avoid posting private addresses for people instead of sites.
No hate speech or coordinated brigading.
Do not upload imagery that sexualises minors or glorifies self harm.

blocks.text: If tempers rise, pause and capture facts only. Moderators prioritise safety over speed.
Block user on profiles or comment threads when someone targets you. For serious threats, contact us with screenshots and URLs.

blocks.title: Escalation paths
items: Block first for persistent harassment in app.
Use Contact for illegal content or imminent harm.
Organisers can remove disruptive event chat participants per house rules.

blocks.text: This article summarises behavior expectations. Binding rules sit in the legal pages linked below.

blocks.title: Where to read more
items: Privacy explains data categories and retention.
Terms explain acceptable use and enforcement levers.
The data page lists rights requests for account holders.
